title: Add handling for system restart in libpod
body: Detect system restart with a file in our temporary directory. When restart is detected, refresh the state in the database to accurately reflect that containers are no longer mounted or running, and recreate containers in runc if necessary.

comments.body: Going to mark this as WIP, because I think @TomSweeneyRedHat is right, the race condition needs to be addressed before merge.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 750fc23) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
Race condition should be fixed. Marking as merge-ready. @rhatdan PTAL.
Travis failure is a flake - Fedora registries were down earlier today

@rhatdan Dropped init() from container refresh. I still feel like this is bad design from an API standpoint, but we can worry about that later. kpod should be fine because Start() always does an init.
@mheon is the test infrastructure still screwed up?
Looks like. Fedora images haven't been pulling most of yesterday and today.
+1 on the Fedora registry being unhappy still.  It's been a real pain.
Some of the things I'm doing to wire in network namespaces are going to make this PR unworkable. Likely going to fold it into the network PR and get everything working there.
I'm going to mark this WIP again. Need to rework so it won't trample on the networking stuff I'm working on.

Alright, this should be ready again. Major changes to add support for networking include moving most of state refresh into the database to better handle network namespaces, and ensuring we always delete and recreate the OCI spec if there was an existing one. We cannot rely on existing specs as paths (most notably network namespaces) may not be the same after a reboot

@TomSweeneyRedHat they should be resolved. We now acquire a file lock to protect checking for the init file (and performing the subsequent state refresh if necessary). This ought to be sufficient to prevent races.
@mheon Thanks for the 411.  There looked to be some open questions still in the review, glad to hear they've been settled.  The file lock idea works for me.
Homu does not seem to be working, merging manually.
